description: Ambleside Online Year 4 — Charlotte Mason living books curriculum for grade 4. First encounters with Plutarch's Lives and Shakespeare; broader world history spanning the 17th–18th centuries; richer, more analytical narration.

books.title: George Washington's World
books.author: Genevieve Foster
books.subject: history
books.notes: Foster sets Washington's life beside what was happening across the whole world at the same time. Great for building a sense of simultaneous history. 'What was happening in another country the same year Washington was a boy?'

books.title: Trial and Triumph: Stories from Church History
books.author: Richard Hannula
books.subject: saints
books.notes: Short, vivid chapters on real believers who held to their faith under real cost. Ask: 'What gave this person the courage to keep going?'

books.title: Plutarch's Lives (Ambleside selections, continuing rotation)
books.author: Plutarch
books.subject: history
books.notes: Continues the Plutarch rotation begun in Year 3, in the original archaic North translation. Read slowly, one incident at a time. 'Was this leader's choice wise, or only brave?'

books.title: Age of Fable (Bulfinch's Mythology)
books.author: Thomas Bulfinch
books.subject: living_books
books.notes: The Greek and Roman myths that shaped centuries of Western literature and art. Not history — story. 'Why do you think people told this story to explain the world?'

books.title: Robinson Crusoe
books.author: Daniel Defoe
books.subject: living_books
books.notes: One man's careful, practical rebuilding of a whole life from nothing. Good for discussing patience and providence. 'What would you have done differently on the island?'

books.title: Tales from Shakespeare: The Merchant of Venice (Lamb's prose retelling)
books.author: Charles and Mary Lamb
books.subject: living_books
books.notes: First taste of Shakespeare, in the Lambs' gentle prose retelling before the full play comes later in the Charlotte Mason rotation. 'Was Portia's solution fair to everyone?'

books.title: Madam How and Lady Why (first half)
books.author: Charles Kingsley
books.subject: science
books.notes: Kingsley explains earth science as a conversation with a curious child. Read a chapter, then go find a real example outdoors if possible. 'What question would you ask Madam How?'

books.title: The Storybook of Science
books.author: Jean-Henri Fabre
books.subject: science
books.notes: Fabre's grandfatherly narrator answers a child's endless nature questions. Excellent read-aloud pacing for this age. 'What did Uncle Paul notice that most people would miss?'

books.title: Gregor Mendel: The Friar Who Grew Peas
books.author: Cheryl Bardoe
books.subject: science
books.notes: A monk-scientist whose quiet, patient counting of pea plants in his monastery garden founded modern genetics. 'Why did it take so many years of counting before Mendel understood the pattern?'

books.title: Handbook of Nature Study (selections)
books.author: Anna Botsford Comstock
books.subject: nature_study
books.notes: A reference, not a read-aloud — dip in for whatever the child has just found outside. Pair every entry with a nature journal sketch. 'Draw what you saw, then look up one thing you didn't know about it.'
